summary: Draft candidate package for HibernateEnabledDefault under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power. The clean Win25H2Clean baseline confirmed the current default, the repo power notes carry an exact docs hit, the shared string batch found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe hit, the shared Ghidra batch produced reviewable xref artifacts, and the guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 completed shell-safe but did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault. A follow-up guest-processed post-boot Procmon trigger batch using powercfg, powercfg /energy, CPU stress, and profile-specific probes also completed shell-safe but still did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault. A later trigger-based ETW follow-up then confirmed the current VMware firmware does not support hibernation, so a real hibernation trigger could not be exercised on this baseline. The new guest-return ETW lane materialized the trace inside the guest and returned a compact summary without copy-back, but it still only reached an exact line without an exact query read.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Docs/power/power.md:154 shows `HibernateEnabledDefault` with observed literal `1` in the repo power notes.
validation_proof.notes: The docs-first triage and phase-0 baseline agree on the current Win25H2Clean baseline value for HibernateEnabledDefault. App surfacing is tracked separately from evidence classification.

setting.name: Hibernate Enabled Default
setting.casual_explanation: Hibernate Enabled Default is a low-level power-manager registry value under the main Power key.
setting.why_people_change_it: Researchers track this value when comparing default hibernation policy behavior against the current live hibernation state.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Cross-layer evidence is strong, but the current clean-baseline runtime package still lacks an exact runtime read for this raw power-manager value and the VM firmware does not support a real hibernation trigger.
setting.professional_notes: Win25H2Clean phase-0 existence confirmed HibernateEnabledDefault on the live baseline with preview value 1. The docs-first triage then tied the value to exact repo documentation hits, led by Docs/power/power.md:154. A shared current-build string batch found an exact hit in C:\Windows\System32\ntoskrnl.exe, and the shared Ghidra batch on the same binary preserved a reviewable xref/decompile artifact set for the docs-first power-control family. A guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log was then rerun from RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329. It completed shell-safe and copied back the guest-processed summary and filtered CSV outputs, but it still did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault on the current baseline. A second guest-processed post-boot Procmon trigger batch then ran from RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 using powercfg queries, powercfg /energy, CPU stress jobs, and profile-specific follow-up commands. That lane produced guest-processed summaries and filtered hit CSVs, but it still did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault on the current baseline. A later trigger-based ETW follow-up showed that `powercfg /hibernate on` returns `The request is not supported` on the current VMware firmware, so the intended hibernation trigger cannot be exercised on this VM baseline. The new guest-return ETW lane then completed fully inside the guest, returned a compact JSON summary through VMware guest variables, and confirmed one exact line hit in the ETW CSV, but still no exact query/read operation for HibernateEnabledDefault.

allowed_values.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The current clean VM baseline exposes HibernateEnabledDefault=1.

targets.notes: This candidate is tracked as a raw kernel-facing value, not as a published end-user tweak profile.

app_current_implementation.provider_source: not currently shipped in the app
app_current_implementation.notes: The current app does not expose HibernateEnabledDefault as a direct tweak or supported UI surface.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline

states.rationale: Keep the currently observed baseline value (1) while this raw control stays in research packaging only.

windows_defaults.notes: Observed directly from the 96-key phase-0 existence batch on Win25H2Clean.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed baseline

states.rationale: Track the observed baseline value without turning it into a shipped preset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the repo to track the live baseline and current evidence for this raw power-manager value.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a supported end-user tweak or a documented Microsoft control surface.
tradeoffs: Keeps the candidate research-only and non-actionable.

evidence.title: Repo power notes for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: Repo power notes contain an exact entry for HibernateEnabledDefault, led by Docs/power/power.md:154.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
evidence.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed HibernateEnabledDefault exists on the clean baseline with observed value 1.

evidence.title: Shared docs-first string triage for current-build ntoskrnl
evidence.summary: Shared string triage found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe string hit for HibernateEnabledDefault.

evidence.title: Shared Ghidra xref batch for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The shared ntoskrnl Ghidra batch produced a reviewable string/xref artifact set for HibernateEnabledDefault; some values resolved naturally while several remained forced-boundary fallback blocks.

evidence.title: Shared clean-baseline guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The guest-processed stepwise Procmon boot log completed shell-safe on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329 but did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault.

evidence.title: Guest-processed post-boot Procmon trigger batch for remaining docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: A guest-processed post-boot Procmon lane ran powercfg queries, powercfg /energy, CPU stress, and profile-specific follow-up commands on RegProbe-Baseline-Clean-20260329. It captured path-level Control\\Power activity but still did not capture an exact runtime read for HibernateEnabledDefault.

evidence.title: Trigger-based ETW follow-up for HibernateEnabledDefault
evidence.summary: A later trigger-based ETW lane confirmed the intended hibernation trigger is not available on the current VMware baseline: `powercfg /hibernate on` failed with `The request is not supported`, so runtime promotion remains environment-limited rather than dead-flagged.

evidence.title: Guest-return ETW trigger follow-up for docs-first power-control values
evidence.summary: The guest-return ETW lane avoided guest-to-host copy-back by parsing tracerpt output inside the guest and returning a compact JSON summary through VMware guest variables. For HibernateEnabledDefault it produced an exact line hit, but still no exact query/read operation.

decision.why: HibernateEnabledDefault is backed by phase-0 baseline existence, exact repo-doc hits, current-build ntoskrnl string corroboration, reviewable Ghidra artifacts, the clean-baseline stepwise Procmon boot log package, a second guest-processed post-boot Procmon trigger batch, and a guest-return ETW follow-up. The ETW guest-return lane produced an exact line hit, but the current VMware firmware still does not support a real hibernation transition, so the record remains decision-gated by an environment limitation rather than by a dead-flag conclusion.
blocking_issues: The current clean-baseline runtime package still does not contain an exact runtime query/read operation for this value.
The current VMware baseline does not support hibernation, so a real hibernation trigger is not available for runtime ETW validation on this VM.
